excerpt: Look, if you really don't know what to make for dessert anymore, then you'll win people over in a jiffy with bread-and-butter pudding. We all have the ingredients in our house. It's long since a shame to make this pudding – it's a love pudding. NEEDED: 1 …

seo.title: Bread-and-butter pudding - asks for more - Marinda Kook

html:
Look, if you really don't know what to make for dessert anymore, then you'll win people over in a jiffy with bread-and-butter pudding. We all have the ingredients in our house. It's long since a shame to make this pudding – it's a love pudding.

NEEDED:

  • 1 loaf sliced white bread – can be a day or two old
  • enough apricot jam and butter to spread generously – don't hold anything back
  • 2 cinnamon sticks
  • 1 litre full-cream milk
  • 250 ml castor sugar
  • 5 ml vanilla essence
  • pinch of salt
  • 6 eggs, separated

NOW:

  1. spread the bread generously with butter and apricot jam – make a sandwichcut it once through into triangles
  2. pack the sandwiches like roof tiles in a greased oven dishtuck the cinnamon sticks in between the bread
  3. whisk the milk, 100ml of the castor sugar, salt, vanilla essence and egg yolks together
  4. pour over the bread and bake for 40 minutes at 180 degrees
  5. meanwhile whisk the egg whites and slowly add the remaining 150ml castor sugar – whisk until glossy (soft-peak stage)
  6. after 40min take the pudding out of the oven and drizzle the egg white mixture on top
  7. bake for another 12 minutes or so

It's delicious. We even had it with extra custard. It's a can't-stop-eating pudding.. Believe me it's going to be a regular on my menu now.
